images.caption: Figure 1:Conceptual comparison of self-evolution paradigms for LLM agents.SEAL closes the loop between policy learning and environment adaptation by using verifier-grounded failure diagnosis as the shared feedback signal.

Figure 2:SEAL training-time co-evolution loop.The agent interacts with an executable tool environment to produce
multi-turn trajectories, which are diagnosed into verifier-grounded
failure types. These diagnoses jointly drive learning-interface
evolution through adapted training observations and policy optimization
through diagnosis-guided trajectory weighting, while keeping tool
semantics and evaluation fixed.

Figure 3:Training dynamics comparing Vanilla RL and SEAL on BFCL V3 validation subsets.We plot validation accuracy across training steps forBase,Missing Functions,Missing Parameters, andLong Context. Shaded regions denote binomial standard error estimated from each 100-sample validation subset.
